items.title: Mexico City Pozole Chain Casa de Tono Explores Sale on Expansion Pitch
items.description: Mexican family-owned restaurant chain La Casa de Toño is seeking a sale in a deal that could top $400 million and set the business up for expansion, people familiar with the matter said.

items.title: Southwest Beats Estimates on Strong Demand, Premium Spending
items.description: Southwest Airlines Co. provided an outlook for the full year that exceeded Wall Street’s projections, citing robust travel demand and spending on the carrier’s premium products, even as operating reve

items.title: Pool Supplier Leslie’s Considers Potential Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
items.description: Pool supplier Leslie’s Inc. is considering a range of strategic options to address its debt load, including a potential Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Brazil Unveils New Relief for Companies Hit by US Tariffs
items.description: Brazil’s government unveiled a relief package worth 18.5 billion reais ($3.7 billion) in credit for exporters hit by new US tariffs, as President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva seeks to shield key sectors

items.title: Financial Services Sector Seen as Focus for Activist Campaigns
items.description: Financial services companies are attracting activist investors who see them as generating cash that could be paid out to investors, said Paul Weiss’ Carmen Lu.

items.title: Tesla Stock at ‘Inflection Point’ as Traders Seek AI Credibility
items.description: As Tesla Inc. is set to report results that many expect will set the tone for the company’s artificial-intelligence ambitions, analysts are scouring charts for clues on where the stock is headed.

items.title: Venezuela Oil: Batistas’ Fluxus Clinches Stake in PDVSA Joint Venture
items.description: Brazil’s billionaire Batista brothers have acquired a stake in an oil production project in Venezuela, the latest deal to emerge in a country that the Trump administration is vowing to reinvigorate af

items.title: Dassault Talks With France on Jet Alternative After Airbus Flop
items.description: France’s Dassault Aviation SA is in talks with French authorities to develop an alternative for the FCAS fighter jet project that was aborted after failed attempts with Airbus SE.

items.title: US Goes All-In on Diesel Production Amid Growing Global Shortage
items.description: US refiners are ramping up diesel production to near-record levels, bucking seasonal trends as the Russia and Iran wars trigger a global supply crunch.

items.title: Wall Street Won’t Give Up on Strategy, Sees 170% Upside
items.description: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. is confronting a collapse of confidence almost everywhere — except among Wall Street analysts.

items.title: Galaxy Digital Seeks $3.5 Billion Debt for Texas Data Center
items.description: Galaxy Digital Inc. is planning to raise about $3.5 billion from a debut junk-bond sale to fund a data center tied to CoreWeave Inc., extending AI borrowing’s push into the riskier corners of the US c

items.title: GE Vernova Falls as Outlook Clashes With ‘Too High’ AI Hopes
items.description: GE Vernova Inc.’s shares slumped after a higher revenue outlook failed to impress investors, a sign that it’s getting harder for companies that sell equipment to data centers to meet the market’s sky-

items.title: Vale Elects Ollie Chairman After Weeks of Governance Turmoil
items.description: Vale SA shareholders elected Manuel Lino Silva de Sousa Oliveira as chairman on Wednesday, concluding weeks of boardroom turmoil sparked by an effort from one of the Brazilian mining giant’s largest i

items.title: US 30-Year Yield Raises Alarm in Longest Run Above 5% Since 2007
items.description: The US 30-year bond yield has held above 5% for the longest stretch since the dawn of the financial crisis, echoing investor concerns about a growing debt pile and sticky inflation.

items.title: S&P 500 Slumps for Fourth Time in Five Days as Oil Prices Jump
items.description: The S&P 500 Index slumped on Wednesday following its best day in weeks, as declines in some corners of Big Tech offset gains elsewhere, including energy as oil prices jumped on the escalation of the U

items.title: Paulson-Backed NovaGold Strikes Deal for Full Ownership of Alaska Mine
items.description: NovaGold Resources Inc., a company backed by hedge-fund billionaire John Paulson, struck a deal to obtain full ownership of the Donlin Gold project in Alaska in a move to control one of the largest un

items.title: Kuwait Sells $6 Billion of Bonds Despite Iran Strikes
items.description: Kuwait raised $6 billion from a three-part dollar bond sale on Wednesday, highlighting resilient demand for the OPEC member’s debt even as it comes under daily missile and drone attacks from Iran.

items.title: Yen Rebounds From Four-Decade Low as BOJ May Hike Rates Faster
items.description: The yen slid beyond 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986 before recovering some ground in choppy trading on the prospect of faster interest-rate hikes from the Bank of Japan.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 22
items.description: Oil climbed, extending this month’s price jump to nearly 30%, as the US and Iran played down the prospect of peace talks and disruptions to global supplies continued to mount.

items.title: Stocks Rise Late as Megacap Earnings Kick Off: Markets Wrap
items.description: Wall Street investors sent stocks higher in late hours as traders sifted through results from megacaps for clues on whether their massive artificial-intelligence investments are paying off.
